t_ch: 你说处死就处死？
t_en: He should be executed just because you say so?

t_ch: 你说处死，问过元老的意见了吗？这案子到底该怎么判，可不是你能决定的，不然我们还站在这搞什么公民法庭？趁早回家算了。
t_en: You speak of execution, but have you asked for the Elder's opinion yet? If you had the final say on this trial's outcome, why would we even need to stand here in front of a public court? Might as well call it a day.

t_ch: 依照圣城法律，偷盗的最高量刑是监禁，确实罪不至死。
t_en: According to the holy city's laws, the heaviest punishment for theft is imprisonment, not execution.

t_ch: 我、我没有跟元老和法律作对的意思……
t_en: I—I have no intention of defying the Elder and the law...

t_ch: 他又没有从中获利。
t_en: He stood to gain nothing from his acts.

t_ch: 卖给外邦人？那群外邦人穷得叮当响，找你销赃都比找他们靠谱——你为富不仁，但确实富啊。
t_en: Selling to outlanders? Those outlanders were penniless — he would've done much better fencing the food to you instead. You may have gotten rich unscrupulously, but the fact is that you are rich.

t_ch: 据圣城护卫查证，没有发生金钱交易的痕迹。请控方代表不要假定事实。
t_en: According to the city guard's findings, there was no evidence of any monetary transaction. I will ask the prosecution not to make any frivolous assumptions.

t_ch: 咳…我们换个话题，换个话题。
t_en: Ahem... Let us change the subject then.

t_ch: 那可是他凭本事偷的。
t_en: It was his own ability that determined the theft's success.

t_ch: 你以为谁都能从圣城仓库里偷东西吗？你们的安保这么不严密？
t_en: You think anyone can waltz into the holy city's warehouse and plunder its supplies? Is your security that lax?

t_ch: 他偷药，既是为了救人，也是为了检验圣城仓库的防御——果然发现问题了吧？你们不想着加强防范，反倒要治他的罪？
t_en: He stole the medicine not only to save lives, but also to ascertain the warehouse's security measures. And there was a problem found, was there not? You never thought to strengthen your security, but seek to punish him instead?

t_ch: 公民们陷入了短暂的沉默，主要是因为你的发言太过「新锐」，没人能理解其逻辑。
t_en: The citizens fall into a temporary silence, mainly because your argument is too avant-garde for them to grasp its logic.

t_ch: …什么歪理？他能凭本事偷药，我们也能凭本事处死他啊。
t_en: ...What rubbish reasoning is this? If he wielded his ability to steal medicine, it is within our ability to sentence him to death.

t_ch: 异邦的英雄，请不要假定事实。这个话题就先过去，控方代表可以继续发言。
t_en: Outlander hero, please do not make any assumptions. We shall pass on this subject for now. Will the prosecution representative continue his argument.

t_ch: 以黄金裔盗取火种打比方。
t_en: Use the Chrysos Heirs stealing Coreflame as an example.

t_ch: 我听说黄金裔以搜集火种、拯救世界为己任，必须向泰坦发起狩猎。苏克提瑞斯的做法和他们相比又有何不同？
t_en: I hear that the Chrysos Heirs saw it their duty to gather Coreflame and save the world, to declare a hunt against the Titans. Is what Soctires did any different from theirs?

t_ch: 倘若为了救人而偷盗是大罪，难道你要说现在的奥赫玛建立在不义之上吗？否则，为什么黄金裔是英雄，苏克提瑞斯却成了罪人？
t_en: If stealing to save lives is such a heinous crime, are you insinuating that the present Okhema was built on injustice? If not, why are the Chrysos Heirs heralded as heroes, but Soctires a criminal?

t_ch: 呵呵，你可不要搞错。那些泰坦们要么陷入疯狂、要么无所作为，这才导致生灵涂炭。
t_en: Heh-heh, don't get it wrong. Those Titans were either mired in madness, or simply did nothing. That was what caused the devastation of life.

t_ch: 黄金裔搜集夺火种，既是为了将泰坦解放，也是为了帮众人博得一线生机。这叫名正言顺。
t_en: The Chrysos Heirs gathered Coreflame to liberate the Titans and pave a possible path for survival for all mortals. It is perfectly justified.

t_ch: 可我们圣城公民哪里对不起外邦人了？我们有害过他们吗？我们只是不让他们进城而已。
t_en: But when have our holy city's citizens mistreated outlanders? Have we harmed them? We merely turned them away at the city gates.

t_ch: 观众们陷入了议论——其中不乏其他泰坦的信徒。而伊特罗听到这个话题则是有些尴尬，不知该如何开口。
t_en: The audience is engrossed in discussion — scattered among them are many disciples of other Titans. And Etro, somewhat embarrassed by this subject, struggles to find his voice.

t_ch: …二位，有关黄金裔和泰坦的话题未免太过敏感，还是别再提及为好……
t_en: ...Both parties, subjects regarding the Chrysos Heirs and Titans may be too sensitive to discuss. It would be best not to pursue this line of reasoning...

t_ch: …退一步说。假如苏克提瑞斯真是善心泛滥，看不得外邦人受苦，那他为什么不自掏腰包？
t_en: ...I'll take a step back and assert this: If Soctires was really overcome with compassion, unwilling to further witness the outlanders' plight, why did he not use his own assets?

t_ch: 就算他捐出全部家当，也不过是他个人的自由。但他不该拿我们全体公民的东西，奥赫玛人的财产只给奥赫玛人用！
t_en: Even if he donated his entire fortune, that would be his personal freedom to do so. But he should not have taken property that belongs to the people. Okhema's property is only for the people of Okhema!

t_ch: 你怎么定义奥赫玛人？
t_en: How do you define an Okheman?

t_ch: 你的意思是，奥赫玛人生来就是奥赫玛人？只要你睁开眼时不在墙内，这辈子就没机会加入圣城了？
t_en: You mean, an Okheman is determined the moment they are born? If, the first time you open your eyes, you aren't within city walls, you'll never have a chance to join the holy city?

t_ch: 别忘了，拯救这里的黄金裔英雄中还有几个外邦人呢！
t_en: Don't forget, the Chrysos Heirs who saved this city contain outlanders!

t_ch: 你…不要质疑我对英雄们的感恩！万敌勇士乃是悬锋城王储，白厄勇士更是命定的救世主。
t_en: You— Do not undermine my gratitude for the heroes! Warrior Mydei is the crown prince of Castrum Kremnos, and Warrior Phainon is the destined Deliverer.

t_ch: <size=28>他们为奥赫玛带来的贡献，一万个外邦人都比不上！</size>
t_en: <size=28>The contributions they have made to Okhema... not even ten thousand outlanders can dream of garnering!</size>

t_ch: 拉莱德这话看似正确，实则是用空话强词夺理，引来了台下鄙夷的目光。
t_en: It may seem that Liluder's words are true, but it is nothing but a strawman argument that has attracted disdainful stares from the onlookers.

t_ch: 反正放着也是放着。
t_en: It's not like the supplies are going anywhere.

t_ch: 物资不就是拿来用的？难不成一直放在仓库里等着发霉？还不如拿去给需要的人呢。
t_en: Aren't supplies supposed to be used? You're going to just let it collect mold in the warehouse? Might as well give it to those who really need it.

t_ch: 嘿，你怕是不知道什么叫战略物资。我可以不用，但不能没有！
t_en: Ha, you don't know the first thing about wartime supplies. I may not need to use them, but they must always be available!

t_ch: 黑潮的威胁仍旧很大，我们必须随时保持战备状态。难道你要让那些与黑潮对抗的战士得不到药吗？
t_en: The black tide's threat looms ever so great. We must always be ready for battle. Are you going to deprive our warriors battling the black tide of precious medicine?

t_ch: 想到这个场景，公民们纷纷表现得义愤填膺。你的发言显然为拉莱德递了刀子……
t_en: Indignation gradually crosses the citizens' faces when they imagine this scenario. Your statement has obviously given Liluder the upper hand...

t_ch: 那你又为奥赫玛出过多少力？
t_en: And how much have you contributed to Okhema?

t_ch: 那在之前的圣城保卫战中，身为奥赫玛人的你又为奥赫玛做了什么贡献呢？
t_en: In the last battle of the holy city's defense, what contributions did you make for Okhema, as an Okheman?

t_ch: 不说亲自上阵作战，你有出资帮助灾后重建吗？你做到过所谓「奥赫玛人的财产只给奥赫玛人用」吗？
t_en: Forget about personal deployment — Did you offer resources to help rebuild in the aftermath? Did you hold true to your previous declaration that "Okhema's property is only for Okhemans"?

t_ch: 你？你、你…真是哪壶不开提哪壶！
t_en: You? You, you... stop your slander!

t_ch: 作为本地富户，拉莱德完美符合对「为富不仁」的所有刻板印象：贪得无厌、趾高气扬、一毛不拔……
t_en: As a local affluent, Liluder perfectly embodies the stereotypical "unscrupulous rich" person: Greedy, pompous, miserly...

t_ch: 观众们回想起拉莱德平日的作为，纷纷开始骂他，直到元老厉声制止。
t_en: Recalling Liluder's usual behavior, the spectators start berating him, till the Elder calls for order.

t_ch: …对案情的讨论不应上升到人身攻击。请两位用更加健康的方式提出观点。
t_en: ...Discussion about the case should not give rise to personal attacks. I will ask both parties to present their arguments in a more cordial manner.

t_ch: 我建议你学习刻法勒的品德。
t_en: I suggest you learn from Kephale's virtues.

t_ch: 照你这么说，刻法勒何必捧着那太阳？他又不需要光照，难道是吃饱了撑的？
t_en: If it is as you say, why did Kephale carry the sun? He doesn't need sunlight. What, he had nothing better to do?

t_ch: 你有没有想过，如果所有人都像你一样自私，谁来将黎明背负？奥赫玛又怎么能成为永恒之城？
t_en: You ever gave a second thought about this? If everyone was as selfish as you, who would bear the burden of dawn? How would Okhema even become the Eternal City?

t_ch: 这话说得直击要害。人们纷纷看向远处的黎明机器，以及将黎明托起的刻法勒——它的精神确实值得学习。
t_en: This point strikes true and draws blood. Heads gradually turn towards the Dawn Device in the distance, and Kephale, who supported the dawn on their shoulders through sheer will — their spirit is truly worth emulating.

t_ch: 这…你总不能要求每个人都那么做。
t_en: This... You can't expect everyone to perform such ridiculous feats.

t_ch: 承认吧，拉莱德，只有你做不到而已。
t_en: Admit it, Liluder, you're the only one who can't live up to this ideal.

t_ch: 好好好…换个话题，换个话题总行吧？
t_en: Fine, fine... Let's change the subject. We can do that, can't we?

t_ch: 谁都知道圣城资源有限，经不起挥霍。苏克提瑞斯把我们的财富交给外邦人，必然会导致公民生活水平的下降。
t_en: Everyone knows the holy city's resources are limited — they can't be expended unnecessarily. Soctires gave our wealth to outlanders. That will surely impact the living standards of our citizens.

t_ch: 在对抗黑潮的战争中，怜悯是无用的品质。为了保卫我们的生活，必须对苏克提瑞斯和他的外邦朋友们不留情面！
t_en: In the war against the black tide, mercy is a useless quality. To safeguard our lives, we must not show mercy to Soctires and his outlander friends!

t_ch: 不要搞错了敌人的身份。
t_en: Do not mistake the enemy's identity.

t_ch: 照你这么说，外邦人也算敌人了？明明我们应该和他们团结对外，一同抵抗黑潮才对。
t_en: So you mean that outlanders are enemies too? We should be united with them against external threats, to fend off the black tide together.

t_ch: 外邦人是没有攻打圣城，但他们也没有守护圣城呀。奥赫玛是因为它的公民才得以幸存，轮不到外邦人来摘桃子！
t_en: The outlanders did not attack the city, but they also did not partake in its defense. Okhema survived because of her citizens — no outlander should deserve to reap her blessings!

t_ch: 听到可能被外人占便宜，在场的公民激动了起来。他们高呼着对拉莱德表示支持，全然没意识到他本人或许才是那个摘桃子的家伙。
t_en: Upon hearing that they might be taken advantage of by outsiders, the present crowd gets riled up. They hoot and holler in support of Liluder, oblivious to the fact that they might just be the ones reaping said blessings.

t_ch: 那战争结束后呢？
t_en: What about after the war, then?

t_ch: 我去过一座名叫雅利洛的城邦，他们还没从对抗风雪的战斗中获胜，却已经有了重建家园的计划——这是你从没考虑过的。
t_en: I went to a city-state called Jarilo, where they had never triumphed in the battle against the storm, yet they already had plans to rebuild their home... This is something you have never once considered.

t_ch: 只要假以时日，外邦人创造的资源总会比消耗的多，公民的生活也会因此改善。难道你们不想看见那一天吗？
t_en: As long as time flows, the resources that outlanders create will one day exceed their consumption, and the citizens' lives will be improved. Do you all not wish to see such a day?

t_ch: 你、你这说法未免太理想化了……
t_en: Y—Your rhetoric reeks of sheer idealism...

t_ch: 拉莱德试图挽回局势，却不能阻止公民们畅想那副画面。是啊，还有什么比团结一致、共创财富更振奋人心呢？
t_en: Liluder's attempts to salvage the momentum, but he can't stop the crowd from imagining that possibility. Yes, what else would inspire people more than a united front and creating shared fortunes?

t_ch: 你不怕外邦人反抗？
t_en: You're not afraid of an outlander revolt?

t_ch: 你该不会以为，只要对外邦人冷眼相待，公民的生活水平就不会下降了吧？
t_en: You aren't presuming that as long you treat the outlanders coldly, the citizens' livelihoods won't be affected, are you?

t_ch: 人是会反抗的。即使人们眼中的弱者，被逼到份上时也会引发冲突。从功利角度看，帮助并安抚他们才是最划算的选择。
t_en: People will revolt. Even the meek will bare their fangs when forced into a corner. From a pragmatic perspective, assisting and placating them is the most economical choice.

t_ch: 那…那是因为圣城一直以来都太过仁慈了。对付这种暴民，就该出重拳才对！
t_en: That... That is because the holy city has been far too magnanimous. An iron fist is required in quashing dissidents!

t_ch: 拉莱德放着狠话，公民们却陷入了沉思。说到底，这不过是在衡量得失罢了，没人愿意见到双输的局面。
t_en: Liluder's harsh words hang in the air, but the citizens are deep in their own thoughts. Fundamentally speaking, it is but a matter of evening out loss and gain — no one wishes for a lose-lose situation.

t_ch: 给他一点小小的「毁灭」震撼。
t_en: Give him a little taste of Destruction.

t_ch: 你冷笑着看向拉莱德，仿佛凶兽在打量一只羔羊。这股威压让他不自觉打了个哆嗦。
t_en: You smile chillingly at Liluder, like a vicious beast sizing up a lamb. He reflexively shudders before your oppressive intent.

t_ch: 你、你要干什么？这里可不是竞技场啊！
t_en: Wh—What are you doing? This isn't an arena!

t_ch: 人早晚是要死的，这座城邦也终会被攻破——我见过太多类似的事了。
t_en: Everyone dies sooner or later. This city will inevitably fall as well. I have seen such similar outcomes too many times.

t_ch: 物资这种东西，死了也带不走。为什么不趁着还拥有当下，多跟别人分享分享呢？
t_en: Supplies can't be taken with you when you're dead. Why shouldn't we seize the present and share them with whomever we can?

t_ch: 此话一出，不光拉莱德，在场公民们的表情也变得无比复杂。你的观点大概不会有人反对，但绝对不会有人支持。
t_en: With these words, not just Liluder, but the expressions of everyone present become indescribably twisted. Your standpoint will probably not be challenged, but it definitely won't be favored by any either.

t_ch: 咳…灰发的英雄，我们只是盟友，你越界了。
t_en: Ahem... Gray-haired hero, we are only allies. You've crossed a line.

t_ch: 随着最后一个音节落地，伊特罗元老伸出手，制止了拉莱德的进一步发言。公民们回味着刚才的交锋，感到热情高涨……
t_en: As the final syllable drops, Elder Etro stretches out his hand and halts Liluder's subsequent elaboration. The citizens recall the exchange that just happened, fervor still pulsing through them...

t_ch: 不知不觉间，你已经代替了帕拉滕的辩方代表之位，而且获得了更多支持。
t_en: Before realizing it, you've replaced Palaten as the defense attorney and garnered more support.

t_ch: 随着最后一个音节落地，伊特罗元老伸出手，制止了拉莱德的进一步发言。公民们回味着刚才的交锋，感到义愤填膺……
t_en: As the final syllable drops, Elder Etro stretches out his hand and halts Liluder's subsequent elaboration. The citizens recall the exchange that just happened, indignation aflame within them...

t_ch: 不知不觉间，你已经代替了帕拉滕的辩方代表之位，但并没有获得多数人的支持。
t_en: Before realizing it, you've replaced Palaten as the defense attorney, but failed to garner the support of the majority.

t_ch: 那么，讨论结束，请各位投票……
t_en: Well, the discussion has concluded. Everyone please cast your vote...

t_ch: 在结束之前，介意我补充几句吗？
t_en: Before we conclude, may I add a few words?

t_ch: 为什么…为什么啊？你不是和我们一起去了重渊吗？你明明亲眼看见了一切，却还是……
t_en: Why... But why? Did you not go with us to the abyss? You clearly witnessed it for yourself, yet...

t_ch: 哎哎，少在那假惺惺的，好像人家跟你多熟一样。说不定{F#她}{M#他}就是因为亲眼看见了才做出这个决定的呢。
t_en: Hey, hey, stop with your crocodile tears and pretending that you're so close. Maybe {F#she}{M#he} made this decision precisely because of what was witnessed.

t_ch: 帕拉滕代表，如果你有异议，还请堂堂正正地驳倒{F#她}{M#他}。
t_en: Representative Palaten, if you have any objections, please openly refute {F#her}{M#him}.

t_ch: 首先，吾师是为了救人性命才出此下策。虽触犯法律，却已是那种情况下的最优解。
t_en: Firstly, my teacher only resorted to such measures in order to save lives. He may have violated the law, but it was the best possible solution under the given circumstances.

t_ch: 希望各位公民在判断时考虑到吾师面对的难题，不要对施助者过于严苛。永恒之城不应该是一座冰冷如铁的城邦。
t_en: I implore the citizens to take into account the challenges my teacher faced when passing judgment, and not to be overly harsh on those who offered aid. The Eternal City is not a cold and iron-hearted state.

t_ch: （现场有这么多人…或许我应该试着调动这些公民的情绪。）
t_en: (So many people present... Maybe I should try to appeal to the public's emotions.)

t_ch: 你觉得自己能代表奥赫玛？
t_en: You think you're qualified to represent Okhema?

t_ch: 圣城应该什么样，是你说了算的吗？你能代表全部奥赫玛人？这座城邦到底是冰冷如铁还是热烈如火，应该由它的公民来评价才对。
t_en: Who are you to decide what the holy city should be? Can you represent all Okhemans? Whether this state is cold as steel or fiery with passion, it's up to its citizens to judge.

t_ch: 我说的是应然，不是实然——你不要混淆这两者的区别！
t_en: I'm talking about what should be, not what is — do not confuse the distinction between the two!

t_ch: 公民们哪有闲心分辨专业词汇的区别？作为「说人话」的奖赏，你得到了如雷的掌声。
t_en: How can the citizens even tell the difference in semantics? As a reward for "speaking the human tongue," you receive thunderous applause.

t_ch: 善恶与对错应该分开看待。
t_en: Good and evil should be viewed separately from right and wrong.

t_ch: 一件事在道德上是善的，不意味着它就是对的，至少不一定符合城邦的利益。
t_en: Just because something is morally good, does not mean it's right. At least, it may not align with the interests of the city.

t_ch: 何况，即使是道德上的善恶也该分开看待。一个人做了善事，不意味着可以让恶行一并消失——这是我从匹诺康尼的二世皇帝身上学到的。
t_en: Also, even the good or evil in morality should be viewed separately. A person's good deeds do not erase their wrongdoings — this is something I learned from the second Emperor of Penacony.

t_ch: 作为外邦英雄，你自述的经历让在场公民坚信不疑、纷纷鼓掌附和。见了这幅景象，帕拉滕只能叹气。
t_en: As an outlander hero, your self-described experiences fully sway the citizens your way, and they chime in with gradual applause. Palaten can only sigh at this sight.

t_ch: 你敢说元老院冰冷如铁？
t_en: You dare insinuate that the Council of Elders is as cold as steel?

t_ch: 你的意思是，现在的圣城和元老院冰冷如铁？真敢说啊。不怕塔兰顿的秤砣砸到你头上？
t_en: You're saying, the holy city and the Council of Elders are as cold as steel? How daring. Are you not afraid that Talanton's weights will come crashing down on your head?

t_ch: 我哪有那个意思？我说的是应然，不是实然——你不要混淆这两者的区别！
t_en: How could I mean that? I'm talking about what should be, not what is — do not confuse the distinction between the two!

t_ch: 咳咳。首先，圣城现在的决策者是阿格莱雅大人。其次，元老院不是那么强权的机构，不会因为几句坏话就拿秤砣砸人。
t_en: Ahem. Firstly, the current decision-maker of the holy city is Lady Aglaea. Secondly, the Council of Elders is not such a tyrannical institution. They will not punish someone with weights over a few disparaging comments.

t_ch: 公民们尽力保持着严肃，但还是能听见几丝笑声从他们憋红的冷脸下窜出——比起对你的认可，更像是看了一出滑稽戏。
t_en: The citizens make an effort to maintain their serious demeanor, but faint laughter still escapes their flushed, yet stern faces — more akin to watching a farce than approval of you.

t_ch: 这不是他犯罪的理由。
t_en: This is not the reason for his crime.

t_ch: 你说的这些，都不足以成为他偷窃的理由。世上身处困境的人那么多，其中又有几个走上了犯罪的道路？
t_en: All that you've said is not enough to justify his theft. There are many people in difficult situations in the world. How many actually choose to turn to a life of crime?

t_ch: 我们不用考虑犯罪的动机，也不需要追溯罪犯悲惨的童年。法律的归法律，道德的归道德，就这么简单。
t_en: We need not consider the motives for the crime, nor investigate the criminal's tragic childhood. Law is law, morality is morality. It's that simple.

t_ch: 那样的城邦…会是多么寒冷的地方啊。
t_en: A city like that... will be such a cold place.

t_ch: 辩方代表，请停止你的情绪化发言，认真对待议题。
t_en: Defense counsel, please cease your emotional statement and address the matter more formally.

t_ch: …另一方面，吾师在开场时已经说过，外邦人并不是一个身份，而是一种处境——我们都可能成为需要帮助的弱者。
t_en: ...On the other hand, my master has said from the start: Outlanders are not an identity, but a circumstance — we all have the possibility of becoming vulnerable individuals in need of aid.

t_ch: 如果吾师的善举不能为他博取减刑，今后还有哪个公民敢冒着风险去帮助他人？为众人抱薪者，不可使其冻毙于风雪。
t_en: If the good deeds my teacher has done can't even earn him a reduced sentence, which citizen will dare take the risk to help others in the future? Those who gather firewood for the masses should not have to freeze to death in the blizzard.

t_ch: 一桩不当判决对社会风气造成的破坏，十个恶人都比不上。公民们，三思啊，不能开这个坏头。
t_en: The damage of a single wrongful ruling on the moral fabric of society is greater than that of ten wrongdoers. Citizens, think carefully — we cannot set such a bad precedent.

t_ch: 你疑似有些小题大做了。
t_en: You're trying to make a mountain out of a molehill.

t_ch: 一桩案子而已，影响哪有你说的那么大。就因为给你的老师定罪，社会风气就要完蛋了？不听你的，圣城就要灭亡了？
t_en: It's just one case. How can the impact be as severe as you claim? Is the moral fabric of our society going to collapse just because your teacher is convicted? If we don't heed your advice, is the entire holy city going to collapse?

t_ch: …难说。
t_en: ...That's difficult to say.

t_ch: 英雄有所不知，公民法庭判案时，确实会考虑到先例的影响。至少在这一点上，辩方代表的担忧不是无中生有。
t_en: Hero, you may not be aware, but it's important to note that when the public court rules on a case, precedent is considered. In this respect, the concerns raised by the defense are not entirely unfounded.

t_ch: 参与法庭讨论是塔兰顿赐予的权利。既然你接受了它，就应当站在城邦与公民的角度思考。这不叫小题大做，这叫权责对等。
t_en: To be able to participate in courtroom discussions is a right granted by Talanton. Since you've accepted it, you are obligated to consider the interests of the state and its citizens. This isn't an exaggeration — it's balancing power and responsibility.

t_ch: 听了元老的话，公民们纷纷低下头、露出若有所思的表情。你的言论大概是不会有人附和了。
t_en: Upon hearing the Elder's words, the citizens lower their heads, their expressions thoughtful. It's likely that no one will support your opinion.

t_ch: 他选错了救人的手段。
t_en: He chose the wrong approach to save others.

t_ch: 苏克提瑞斯的罪行不是救人，而是偷窃。他本可以用更合法的手段提供帮助，比如捐出自己的财产……
t_en: The crime of Soctires is not saving others, but theft. He could have used legal methods to offer aid, such as donating his own property...

t_ch: 我们不是在惩罚他的动机，只是在惩罚他的行为——不这么做才是对社会风气的破坏。
t_en: We are not punishing his motives, but his actions. Failing to hold him accountable will undermine societal values.

t_ch: 你的发言逻辑严密。直到掌声响起，帕拉滕也没能给出一句反驳。
t_en: Your argument is logically sound. Until the applause erupts, Palaten is unable to provide any counterargument.

t_ch: 破坏社会风气？他也干了。
t_en: Undermine societal values? He has done so already.

t_ch: 我同意你说的，不能开这个坏头。那你可知道，他的行为也对社会风气造成了坏影响？
t_en: I agree with you. We should not set such a poor precedent. But are you aware that his actions also negatively impacted societal values?

t_ch: 倘若为苏克提瑞斯网开一面，你就会见到更多「迫不得已」的犯罪者，甚至可能会有人偷药去救治大地兽……
t_en: If we grant Soctires leniency, you will encounter more "compelled" criminals. There may be someone who might even steal medicine to save the dromases...

t_ch: …怎么可能到那种地步呢？你这是滑坡谬误！
t_en: ...How could it possibly come to that? This is a slippery slope argument!

t_ch: 公民们听不懂帕拉滕的学术词语，但「偷药喂给大地兽」的画面已经烙印在了他们脑海中。有人笑出了声。
t_en: The citizens can't understand Palaten's academic term, but the image of "stealing medicine to feed dromases" has already been imprinted in their minds. Some of them even chuckle out loud.

t_ch: 他还欠我钱没还呢！
t_en: He still hasn't returned the money he owes me!

t_ch: 还记得当初我和你们一起去重渊，你老师让我给外邦人买粮食的事吗？他可没说这要我自掏腰包！
t_en: Do you remember when I went with you two to the abyss, and your teacher asked me to buy food for the outlanders? He never mentioned I had to pay for it myself!

t_ch: 他欠我的钱到底什么时候能还？这难道就不是对社会风气的破坏吗？
t_en: When is he going to return the money he owes me? Isn't this also undermining societal values?

t_ch: 如果吾师真被判了重罪，那笔钱估计也……
t_en: If my master is truly convicted of a serious crime, I think that money will likely...

t_ch: 公民们发出一串哄笑——德高望重的苏克提瑞斯借钱不还，还有比这更大的乐子吗？
t_en: The citizens erupt in laughter — what could be more amusing than the esteemed Soctires not returning money he owes?

t_ch: …各位别忘了，黑潮的威胁尚未远去，预言中的末日就在前方。我们要面对的是一场天灾，唯有团结才能为搏来一线生机。
t_en: ...Let's not forget that the threat of the black tide is far from over. The prophesied apocalypse looms. We are facing a natural disaster, and only through unity do we stand a chance at survival.

t_ch: 人拥有无限的可能性。终有一日，我们会战胜黑潮。到了那时，外邦人会成为帮助我们重建家园的伙伴。难道各位愿意扼杀那个可能性吗？
t_en: Humanity holds boundless potential. There will come one day when we prevail over the black tide. When that time comes, the outlanders will stand with us as allies, to rebuild our homeland. Is everyone here willing to extinguish that possibility?

t_ch: 想要放眼未来，必先考虑当下。
t_en: To look forward to the future, one must first consider the present.

t_ch: 如果不考虑眼前的问题，圣城根本走不到未来。你只是在用童话般的描述画饼罢了。
t_en: If we do not consider the issues at hand, the holy city will not see a future. You're just painting a pretty picture no different from a fairy tale.

t_ch: 战胜黑潮？那是很久以后的事。至少现在，圣城不需要外邦人也足够热闹。
t_en: Defeating the black tide? That's in the distant future. For now, the holy city is lively enough without the outlanders.

t_ch: 公民们连连鼓掌称是。在他们看来，永恒之城只可能从内部被攻破——坚决不能让这种事发生。
t_en: The citizens applaud in agreement. To them, the Eternal City can only be breached from within — such a thing cannot be allowed to happen.

t_ch: 是奥赫玛人保卫了城市。
t_en: It's the Okhemans who protected the city.

t_ch: 如果外邦人真是我们的伙伴，为什么奥赫玛被疯王眷属围攻时他们没来？
t_en: If the outlanders are really our allies, why did they not come to our aid when the Mad King attacked Okhema?

t_ch: 是黄金裔和公民们保卫了这座城市——当然，还有我和丹恒。谁出力，谁受益，这不是理所当然的吗？
t_en: It was the Chrysos Heirs and the citizens who protected this city — and of course, together with Dan Heng and I. Isn't it only fair for those who contributed to also reap the benefits?

t_ch: 你这番话点燃了现场的气氛。人们高喊着你的名字，仿佛回到了刀光剑影的战场。
t_en: Your words ignite the atmosphere in the area. The crowd shouts your name, as if they have returned to a battlefield clashing with swords.

t_ch: 你先接几个外邦人回家再说吧。
t_en: Why don't you accept a few outlanders first, then get back to me?

t_ch: 既然你这么关心那些外邦人，为什么不自己接几个回家？
t_en: Since you care so much about the outlanders, why not bring a few of them home?

t_ch: 说来惭愧，我和吾师的宅邸确实收留了一些外邦人。数量不多，也就十个，远远不足以拯救所有需要帮助的人。
t_en: I'm ashamed to admit that my teacher and I have taken in a few outlanders in our homes. Not many, just ten. Far from the number of people who need help.

t_ch: 但我相信这微小的努力也会产生影响，正如水滴也能贯穿顽石。
t_en: But I believe that this tiny effort can make a difference, just like how water drops will eventually wear through stone.

t_ch: 公民们动容了——善行远比善心更加珍贵，人们用掌声表达了对这位学者的钦佩。
t_en: The citizens are deeply moved — good deeds are more precious than good intentions. They express their admiration for the scholar with applause.

t_ch: 外邦人只能当我的啦啦队。
t_en: The outlanders can only be my cheerleaders.

t_ch: 要说对付黑潮，我和我的黄金裔朋友们才是专家——还记得我们从疯王眷属手里拯救圣城的故事吗？那里面可没有外邦人出场。
t_en: If we're talking about defeating the black tide, my Chrysos Heir friends and I are the experts — do you still remember how we saved the holy city from the clutches of the Mad King's kins? There are no outlanders in that party.

t_ch: 你所谓的外邦伙伴能帮上我们什么？举办庆功宴会？还是站在旁边加油喝彩？这活「粉霞天女」也能干。
t_en: What can your so-called outlander buddies do to help us? Host a victory banquet? Or stand by and cheer us on? Even the Rosy Celestial Maiden can do that.

t_ch: 不等帕拉滕发话，在场的公民们就已经笑作了一团。看架势，他不会有机会开口了。
t_en: Without waiting for Palaten's reply, the citizens are already bursting into laughter. By the looks of it, he probably isn't going to get a chance to speak.

t_ch: 随着最后一个音节落地，伊特罗元老伸出手，制止了帕拉滕的进一步发言。公民们回味着刚才的交锋，感到义愤填膺……
t_en: As the final syllable drops, Elder Etro stretches out his hand and halts Palaten's subsequent elaboration. The citizens recall the exchange that just happened, indignation aflame within them...

t_ch: 不知不觉间，你已经代替了拉莱德的控方代表之位，而且获得了更多支持。
t_en: Before you realize it, you've replaced Liluder as the prosecutor and garnered more support.

t_ch: 随着最后一个音节落地，伊特罗元老伸出手，制止了帕拉滕的进一步发言。公民们回味着刚才的交锋，渐渐动了恻隐之心……
t_en: As the final syllable drops, Elder Etro stretches out his hand and halts Palaten's subsequent elaboration. The citizens recall the clash, and begin to feel compassion...

t_ch: 不知不觉间，你已经代替了拉莱德的控方代表之位，但并没有获得多数人的支持。
t_en: Before you realize it, you've replaced Liluder as the prosecutor, but failed to gather the support of the majority.

t_ch: 那么，讨论结束，请各位投票……
t_en: Well, the discussion has concluded. Everyone please cast your vote...

t_ch: 在结束之前，介意我补充几句吗？
t_en: Before we conclude, may I add a few words?

t_ch: …阿格莱雅？
t_en: ...Aglaea?

t_ch: 你好，元老伊特罗。我来得应该还不算太迟。
t_en: Hello, Elder Etro. I think I'm not too late.

t_ch: 阿格莱雅的出现让本已沉寂的观众再次激动起来——很少有民事案件能引来黄金裔的关注。
t_en: Aglaea's appearance spurs the spectators once more — few civil cases can catch the attention of the Chrysos Heirs.

t_ch: 各位先前的讨论，风儿已顺着金丝为我转述。在这个非常时刻，比起争辩衣服上的漏洞是对是错，或许我们更应该考虑怎么去缝补它。
t_en: Everyone's earlier discussions have been conveyed to me through golden threads riding the winds. At such a critical time, instead of arguing over whether the holes in a garment are right or wrong, perhaps we should consider how we should mend them instead.

t_ch: 若不惩罚苏克提瑞斯，便无以向律法和人民交代；但若判罚过重，又会显得圣城不近人情、独断专横。
t_en: Failing to punish Soctires, it will be unjust to the people and the law. But if the punishment is too harsh, it will cause the holy city to be seen as cold and tyrannical.

t_ch: 所以，想要在这两条金丝之间找到一个平衡点，最好的选择是……
t_en: Therefore, to strike a fair balance between the two, the best choice is...

t_ch: 监禁改为流放？
t_en: Change imprisonment to exile?

t_ch: 派去义务劳动？
t_en: Assign him community service?

t_ch: 让他赔偿损失？
t_en: Make him pay for the losses?

t_ch: 我们的贵客给出了不错的思路。顺着{F#她}{M#他}的话头，我来为各位公民提出一个折衷方案——
t_en: Our honored guest has offered some good ideas. Following {F#her}{M#his} thoughts, I would like to offer a compromise to the citizens...

t_ch: 苏克提瑞斯将免于监禁。作为替代，他会被派去难民庇护所务工，期间视作流放；除此以外，他还必须自费填补药物储备的空缺。
t_en: Soctires shall be exempt from imprisonment. As an alternative, he will be assigned to work at a refuge shelter, as a form of exile. Additionally, he will have to personally bear the cost of replenishing the medical supplies.

t_ch: 对某些公民而言，这样的惩罚甚至比在监狱服刑还要可怕，无须担心有人效仿。
t_en: To some citizens, such punishment might be more daunting than spending time in prison, so there's no need to worry about others following suit.

t_ch: 我要补充的就是这些，各位可以开始投票了。
t_en: This is all I have to add. Everyone, please begin voting.

t_ch: 请等一下！阿格莱雅大人，我还有一个疑问——您打算如何处置那些外邦人？
t_en: Please hold! Lady Aglaea, I still have a question — how do you intend to deal with that bunch of outlanders?

t_ch: 阿格莱雅脸上闪过一丝不易察觉的笑意，仿佛她一直在等待这句话。
t_en: A faint smile crosses Aglaea's face, as if she has been anticipating this question all along.

t_ch: 外邦人的命运，决定权不在我或者元老院，而是在各位公民手中。
t_en: The fate of the outlanders does not lie with me nor the Council of Elders, but with the citizens.

t_ch: 如果你支持他们，就确实去伸出援手。如果你反对他们，就当面把话讲出来。即使没有被金丝相连，表达心意也绝非难事。
t_en: If you support them, offer a helping hand. If you oppose them, speak directly. Even if you are not connected by golden threads, expressing one's mind shouldn't be too hard.

t_ch: 正如这位老者，他的态度就很鲜明，他的行动也确实产生了影响。有人因此受益，有人因此受罚……
t_en: Just like this old one. His stance is clear. His actions have indeed made an impact. Some have benefited, while others have faced punishment...

t_ch: 倘若每一位公民都能像他这般，那么在智慧与意志的交锋中，终会有一条清晰的道路显现。
t_en: If every citizen was like him, with the clash of wisdom and will, a clear path would emerge.

t_ch: 现在，是你们表达态度的时候了。请继续吧，伊特罗元老。
t_en: Now, it's time to declare your stance. Please continue, Elder Etro.

t_ch: 那么，请各位公民举手表决……
t_en: Then, citizens, please raise your hand to vote...

t_ch: 最终，六成的票数支持流放苏克提瑞斯…
t_en: Finally, sixty percent of the votes support Soctires' exile...

t_ch: 卫兵们押着这位老者远去。他和弟子很快就会收拾行囊、离开圣城…
t_en: The guards escort the old man away. Both he and his student quickly pack up their belongings and leave the holy city...

t_ch: 广场上的公民越来越少，人们回味着刚刚发生的一切。这座城市究竟该去往何方？还没有人能给出答案。
t_en: The citizens at the plaza start to scatter, with people digesting what just happened. In what direction should this city head towards? No one seems to have the answer.

t_ch: 但至少，它会去往明天。
t_en: But at the very least, it heads towards tomorrow.

t_ch: 我旁听了你的全部发言，贵客，也许你比我想象中更适应辩论场。
t_en: I heard your entire speech, honored guest. Perhaps you're more suited to be in the debate arena than I imagined.

t_ch: 但参与这种讨论是有风险的…民意就像一把火，没人能确定它会被引向何方。
t_en: But participating in such a debate has its risks... Public opinion is like an open flame, no one can predict where it will be directed.

t_ch: 是你啊，贵客。没能见到你发言，有点可惜。
t_en: It's you, honored guest. It was a pity that I was unable to witness your speech.

t_ch: 但这未必不是件好事。群众有时会被不那么明智的声音裹挟，而你不会成为其中一员。
t_en: But it may not be a bad thing. The crowd may be swayed by less wise voices, but you will not be one of them.

t_ch: 重渊的外邦人怎么样了？
t_en: What happened to the outlanders at the Abyss?

t_ch: 圣城守卫们接走了所有能找到的外邦人——当然，没有带进城区，只是送到了避难所。这是我能给予的最大仁慈。
t_en: The guards of the holy city escorted all the outlanders who could be found. Of course, they were not brought within the city walls, but to the shelter. This is the greatest mercy I can offer.

t_ch: 外邦人的事，你怎么看？
t_en: What's your view on the outlander situation?

t_ch: 哦？我还以为自己在庭上已经说得很清楚了呢。但有些话确实不能暴露在阳光下……
t_en: Oh? I thought I expressed it quite clearly in court. Though some words cannot be exposed to the light of day...

t_ch: 圣城的资源有限，这个事实不因我们的态度而改变。奥赫玛帮不了所有人。
t_en: The resources of the holy city are limited. This truth cannot be changed by our attitudes. Okhema can't help everyone.

t_ch: 只要黑潮的威胁还存在一天，这样的悲剧就不会结束…所以，我们必须狩猎火种。
t_en: As long as the threat of the black tide exists for another day, this tragedy will not cease... Hence, we have to hunt down all the Coreflame.

t_ch: 之后再聊吧。
t_en: Let's chat next time.

t_ch: 嗯，贵客，我们都还有要做的事，再见。
t_en: Indeed, honored guest. We still have much to do. Farewell.

t_ch: 还有什么要问的事吗，贵客？
t_en: Is there anything else you'd like to ask, honored guest?

t_ch: 重渊的外邦人怎么样了？
t_en: What happened to the outlanders at the Abyss?

t_ch: 圣城守卫们接走了所有能找到的外邦人——当然，没有带进城区，只是送到了避难所。这是我能给予的最大仁慈。
t_en: The guards of the holy city escorted all the outlanders who could be found. Of course, they were not brought within the city walls, but to the shelter. This is the greatest mercy I can offer.

t_ch: 外邦人的事，你怎么看？
t_en: What's your view on the outlander situation?

t_ch: 哦？我还以为自己在庭上已经说得很清楚了呢。但有些话确实不能暴露在阳光下……
t_en: Oh? I thought I expressed it quite clearly in court. Though some words cannot be exposed to the light of day...

t_ch: 圣城的资源有限，这个事实不因我们的态度而改变。奥赫玛帮不了所有人。
t_en: The resources of the holy city are limited. This truth cannot be changed by our attitudes. Okhema can't help everyone.

t_ch: 只要黑潮的威胁还存在一天，这样的悲剧就不会结束…所以，我们必须狩猎火种。
t_en: As long as the threat of the black tide exists for another day, this tragedy will not cease... Hence, we have to hunt down all the Coreflame.

t_ch: 之后再聊吧。
t_en: Let's chat next time.

t_ch: 嗯，贵客，我们都还有要做的事，再见。
t_en: Indeed, honored guest. We still have much to do. Farewell.

t_ch: 浴场这么大…那位老者的浴池在哪呢？
t_en: The bath pool is huge... Where is that old man's bath?

t_ch: 你们快到神殿里去，那边安全——跟我来。
t_en: You all hurry to the temple. It's safe there — follow me.

t_ch: 这里有许多食品店，价钱和质量都有区别。该选哪个呢……
t_en: There are many food stalls here, varying in price and quality. Which one to choose...

t_ch: 这样食物的事就算解决了，去找外邦人领队吧。
t_en: Now that the food is settled, it's time to find the outlander leader.

t_ch: 帕…帕里斯……
t_en: Pa... Paris...

t_ch: 我、我不认识什么帕里斯！别伤害我和「赫拉克勒斯」！
t_en: I—I don't know any Paris! Don't hurt me or Heracles!

t_ch: 嗡！嗡嗡！
t_en: Roar! Roaaaar!

t_ch: 退下！该死的{RUBY_B#尼卡多利}疯王{RUBY_E#}残兵，不许再向前了！
t_en: Stay back! Foul remnant of {RUBY_B#Nikador}the Mad King{RUBY_E#}. You are not permitted any further!
Kill...

t_ch: 你谁也杀不了！
t_en: You will kill nobody!

t_ch: 球棒侠来也！
t_en: The baseballer is here!

t_ch: 闪！
t_en: Out of the way!

t_ch: 是谁？这里很危险，快点离开！
t_en: Who are you? This place is dangerous. Get out of here at once!

t_ch: 等等，{F#她}{M#他}好像是与黄金裔们一起狩杀疯王的英雄！
t_en: Wait, I think {F#she}{M#he} was the hero that hunted down the Mad King with the Chrysos Heirs!

t_ch: 什么？太好了，请快帮帮我们吧！
t_en: What? Perfect! Please, come and help us!

t_ch: 有事我真上！
t_en: You can count on me!

t_ch: 有奖励吗？
t_en: Is there a reward?

t_ch: 这…{F#她}{M#他}真的是英雄吗？
t_en: Uh... Are you sure {F#she}{M#he}'s a hero?

t_ch: 哎呀，有好处！我是商队的领队，好处有的是！
t_en: Jeez, you will be rewarded! I am the leader of a merchant caravan, and I have all the rewards you could need!

t_ch: 看来不得不上了。
t_en: Looks like I have no choice.

t_ch: 求我。
t_en: Beg me.

t_ch: 啊？你在说什么？
t_en: Huh? What are you saying?

t_ch: 求你了，请出手吧！
t_en: I'm begging you. Please do something!

t_ch: 看来不得不上了。
t_en: Looks like I have no choice.

t_ch: {RUBY_B#泰坦詈语}Hinas{RUBY_E#}……
t_en: {RUBY_B#Titan curses}Hinas{RUBY_E#}...

t_ch: 打不过就骂街？
t_en: Can't beat me, so you resort to foul language?

t_ch: 讲素质，做文明眷属。
t_en: Have some class and be a civilized Titankin.

t_ch: *无名客詈语*！
t_en: *Nameless curses*!

t_ch: 面对无法对抗的力量，受创的泰坦眷属转身逃离，遁入城外的永夜天帷。
t_en: Faced with an unstoppable power, the injured Titankin turns and flees, slipping into the Evernight Veil outside the city.

t_ch: 那副踉跄的破碎身躯甚是狼狈，完全引不起胜者追击的欲望，所以你只是凝望着她的影子消逝在光芒一同消逝的境界线外……
t_en: Its staggering and broken form is so pathetic that you lose all urge to chase it down for a victorious counterattack. So, you simply watch as her shadow disappears into the border where the light vanishes altogether.

t_ch: 「天谴之矛」麾下居然有败逃的懦夫，真是可笑…还是多亏英雄出手相助，我们才能全身而退。
t_en: The fact that the Lance of Fury underlings would be so cowardly as to flee in defeat. Pathetic... Thank you for saving us, hero. We would not have made it out of here alive without you.

t_ch: 自从蛮神陨落，这只眷属就时常在附近徘徊，袭击戍卫与过往的商队，这次倒霉的就是「阿尔戈商队」的老板伊阿宋。
t_en: Ever since the fall of the savage god, this Titankin has been roaming the surrounding area, attacking guards and passing trade caravans. This time, it was Iason, leader of the Argo Caravan, who fell victim.

t_ch: 是啊，还好你和克米昂长官及时出手！要是让我去见了塞纳托斯，可就没人能养活城里的商人和大地兽了！
t_en: That's right. Fortunately, you and Captain Camilla took action in time! If I were sent to meet Thanatos, no one would be left to support the city's merchants or dromases!

t_ch: 嗡嗡……
t_en: Groan...

t_ch: 好啦，赫拉克勒斯…唉，虽然保住了小命，但宝物被她抢走了……
t_en: It's okay, Heracles... Ah, though I've been spared my life, she did get away with my little treasure...

t_ch: 有宝物？不早说！
t_en: There's a treasure? Why didn't you say sooner!

t_ch: 刚刚没掉落什么稀有材料啊。
t_en: I didn't see any rare loot just now?

t_ch: 我就知道这任务没完事儿。
t_en: I knew this mission wouldn't just end like that.

t_ch: 那东西可不一般，是{RUBY_B#浪漫的泰坦}墨涅塔{RUBY_E#}的圣物——她亲手创造的<color=#dbc291ff>蜕生金丝</color>！
t_en: This is no mere ordinary object. It's a divine object of {RUBY_B#Romance Titan}Mnestia{RUBY_E#}. It is the <color=#dbc291ff>Golden Thread of Rebirth</color> that she weaved herself!

t_ch: 你听说过这个故事吧——在造物世，墨涅塔将世上的爱与自己的残茧捻作丝线，作为赠予{RUBY_B#理性的泰坦}瑟希斯{RUBY_E#}的礼物。
t_en: You must have heard this story? During the Era Creatura, Mnestia wove together the love of the world and their own cocoon to create silk as a gift to {RUBY_B#Reason Titan}Cerces{RUBY_E#}.

t_ch: 阿格莱雅女士曾出资委托我收集泰坦的神迹，我费尽心思才从游牧的贼团手中购得此物，结果到了城前却被敌人抢走了…真是可惜……
t_en: Lady Aglaea sent me on an assignment to retrieve the miracles of the Titans. I worked myself to the bone to purchase this object from a group of nomadic thieves. Now, it's been snatched from my hands by the enemy right before I arrived back at the city... Such a shame...

t_ch: 伊阿宋低头哀叹，露出遗憾的神色，看似在独自咀嚼痛失宝物的悲伤与辜负阿格莱雅的愧疚——但你能察觉到他眼神在地面和你的身上悄悄摇摆。
t_en: Iason drops his head with a sigh, his face painted with regret. It seems he is stuck between the sadness of having lost the precious item and the guilt of disappointing Aglaea... However, you sense his gaze darting between the ground's surface and your body.

t_ch: 我懂，让我来吧。
t_en: I understand. I'll do it.

t_ch: 你直说要我帮忙不就行了。
t_en: You could just ask me for help.

t_ch: 真是可惜，拜拜。
t_en: What a shame. Bye bye!

t_ch: 等、等等！英雄！这个宝贝真的很宝贵，就请你帮帮忙吧——就当是为了阿格莱雅女士！
t_en: W—Wait! Hero! This treasure is truly extremely precious, and you have to help me! Count it as a favor to Lady Aglaea!

t_ch: 有话直说嘛。
t_en: Just say that next time.

t_ch: 我有空就帮。
t_en: I'll help if I get time.

t_ch: 太好了，谢谢英雄，谢谢英雄！
t_en: That's wonderful. Thank you, hero! Thank you, hero!

t_ch: 那个…我在外行商时听流民们说过，泰坦眷属都聚集在黑潮下的<color=#dbc291ff>悬锋城</color>，只有你们这些英雄知道它在哪。
t_en: So... While I was traveling and selling my wares, I heard word from the refugees that the Titankin were gathering in <color=#dbc291ff>Castrum Kremnos</color> under the black tide. Only a hero such as yourself knows where they are.

t_ch: 英雄，城外黑潮凶险，我们普通人也帮不上什么忙，只能请您多加小心了。
t_en: Hero, as the black tide outside the city is perilous, we mere citizens can offer you no assistance. All we can do is ask you to take great caution.

t_ch: 寻宝！伙伴，一起去寻宝！
t_en: A treasure hunt! Partner, let's go on a treasure hunt!

t_ch: 这群泰坦眷属就聚集在<color=#dbc291ff>悬锋城</color>中，也只有你们这些英雄知道它在哪，拜托了！
t_en: These Titankin are gathered in <color=#dbc291ff>Castrum Kremnos</color>. Only a hero such as yourself knows where they are.

t_ch: 英雄，请务必小心。
t_en: Hero, please be careful.

t_ch: 先前的眷属在与你鏖战一番过后，已经没有了先前的杀气。
t_en: After their ferocious battle with you, the Titankin from before seem to have lost their previous murderous intent.

t_ch: 你此时才发现这副石铸的身躯是多么古老，如夜幕下的悬锋城墙一样陈旧而残破。
t_en: It's at this moment that you realize just how ancient its stone-cast body is. It's just as old and derelict as the walls of Castrum Kremnos under the curtain of night.

t_ch: 帕…里斯……
t_en: Pa... ris...

t_ch: 把金丝交出来！
t_en: Hand over the golden thread!

t_ch: 帕里斯到底是谁？
t_en: Who is this "Paris"?

t_ch: 对对对，我是帕里斯。
t_en: That's right. I'm Paris!

t_ch: …不…你不是……
t_en: ...No... You are not him...

t_ch: 金丝…帕里斯的…被偷走了……
t_en: The golden thread... Paris'... It was stolen...

t_ch: 咦？难道这本来就是你的东西吗？
t_en: Huh? Are you saying this belongs to you?

t_ch: 是…帕里斯……
t_en: It's... Paris...

t_ch: 看来不找到这个叫帕里斯的人，她是不会把宝贝交出来了…那这位帕里斯身在何处呢？
t_en: Looks like she won't hand over the treasure unless I find this "Paris" person... Where could he be?

t_ch: 去了…阿卡迪亚……
t_en: He went... To Arcadia...

t_ch: 好！出发吧，去阿卡迪亚找他…等等，阿卡迪亚是哪里呀？
t_en: Alright! Off we go! To Arcadia to find... Wait — where is Arcadia?

t_ch: 地图上没写。
t_en: It's not on the map.

t_ch: 传送锚点没开。
t_en: We didn't unlock that teleport anchor.

t_ch: 要不给我开个任务指引？
t_en: Want to give me a mission guide?

t_ch: 帕…帕里斯……
t_en: Pa... Paris...

t_ch: 眷属的声音逐渐变得微弱，她残存的神智似乎无法再与你们进行问答，只能默默重复着那个陌生的名字。
t_en: The Titankin's voice is growing ever weaker. It seems she no longer has the mental capacity to answer your questions, only to silently repeat that stranger's name to herself.

t_ch: 真是难办，不认识的人，不认识的地方。
t_en: Well now we're stuck! We don't know this person, or this place!

t_ch: 往世复现！
t_en: We'll go back to the past!

t_ch: 回到她的过去找线索吗？是个好办法！
t_en: Go back to her past to find clues? That's a great idea!

t_ch: 钟表把戏，启动…
t_en: Clockwork, activate...

t_ch: 钟表把戏…是什么东西？我不会呀！
t_en: Clockwork... What is that? I can't do that!

t_ch: 但如果回到她的过去，或许能找到一些线索……
t_en: But, if we go back to her past... Maybe we can find some clues...

t_ch: 直接动手抢走不行吗？
t_en: Can't we just steal it from her now?

t_ch: 不要乘人之危！而且人家能感觉到她没有说谎，还是让我们事情搞清楚再说吧。
t_en: Don't take advantage of those in need! Plus, I can sense that she isn't lying. Let's find out what's going on first and then go from there.

t_ch: 但如果回到她的过去，或许能找到一些线索……
t_en: But, if we go back to her past... Maybe we can find some clues...

t_ch: 不过这位雕像小姐的记忆太过模糊，还需要其他的碎片才能窥见与之相关的往世。伙伴，去云石市集收集一些关于「帕里斯」和「阿卡迪亚」的记忆吧！
t_en: But, this statue lady's memory is too blurry. We need to find other fragments of it if we want to see into her past. Partner, let's go to Marmoreal Market and collect some memories related to "Paris" and "Arcadia"!

t_ch: 你好呀，小朋友，是来买水果的吗？这批货是刚从「花园」送来的，新鲜得很！
t_en: Hello, young friend! Are you here to buy fruits? These are all in fresh from the "garden," and they're fresh as can be!

t_ch: 我想问你一些问题。
t_en: I want to ask you some questions.

t_ch: 问题？哈哈，离开智慧花园之后，已经很久没人需要我解惑了，请问吧。
t_en: Questions? Haha, no one has come to me seeking enlightenment since I left the Garden of Wisdom. Please, ask away.

t_ch: 你认识帕里斯吗？
t_en: Do you know Paris?

t_ch: 帕里斯？我从来没听过这个名字。
t_en: Paris? I have never heard that name before.

t_ch: 我猜他肯定不住在奥赫玛——这圣城上下的人都光顾过我的店铺，每个客人的名字我都记着呢！
t_en: I am certain that he doesn't live in Okhema. People from all over this holy city have frequented my store and I remember the name of every customer!

t_ch: 你听说过阿卡迪亚吗？
t_en: Have you heard of Arcadia?

t_ch: 阿卡迪亚？是什么水果的名字吗？不好意思啊，小朋友，我这不卖阿卡迪亚。
t_en: Arcadia? Is that a fruit? Sorry, young one, I don't sell Arcadia here.

t_ch: 但如果你能搞来它的种子，我就去让智慧花园的老朋友种点，明年你就能买到了！
t_en: But if you can find some seeds, then I can ask my friend in the Garden of Wisdom to plant some. Then you can come and buy some next year!

t_ch: 好像没问到什么有用的东西，再去问问其他店主吧……
t_en: Looks like she didn't have anything useful to offer. Let's go and ask another store owner...

t_ch: 赞美瑟希斯，愿你令圣城子民早些理解我作品的魅力……
t_en: Praise Cerces! May you assist the people of this holy city in understanding the charm of my works...

t_ch: 啊，有顾客！我刚才在向瑟希斯祈求赐予同胞们智慧，想买什么书？
t_en: Ah, a customer! I was just praying to Cerces to gift intelligence to my fellow citizens. Is there a book you are after?

t_ch: 我想问你一些问题。
t_en: I want to ask you some questions.

t_ch: 当然可以，传递知识正是瑟希斯交由我们家族的使命！我知无不言！
t_en: Of course! Imparting knowledge is the very mission entrusted to my family by Cerces! I will share any knowledge I have!

t_ch: 你认识帕里斯吗？
t_en: Do you know Paris?

t_ch: 帕里斯…帕里斯……我居然不知道这个名字？你等等！
t_en: Paris... Paris... I've never heard that name? Wait!

t_ch: 克帖西芬在石板与书卷中翻找了许久，看来石板和卷轴做书最不方便的就是没有封面辨别书名……
t_en: Ctesiphona rummages for some time among slates and scrolls. It seems that the most inconvenient thing about working with slates and scrolls is that there is no front cover to help identify them quickly...

t_ch: 呼——无论是诗歌、神话、戏剧还是冒险故事里都没有这个人。看来我帮不上你的忙了。
t_en: Phew... I checked poems, mythology, plays, and adventures, but no mention of this person. Looks like I won't be able to help you.

t_ch: 你听说过阿卡迪亚吗？
t_en: Have you heard of Arcadia?

t_ch: 阿卡迪亚？客人，你真是位阅历不浅的读者，这可是古老年代的冒险故事最爱引用的设定——<color=#dbc291ff>墨涅塔庇护的黄金之乡</color>！
t_en: Arcadia? Dear customer, I see you must be a most experienced reader. Arcadia is a common setting people love to use in ancient adventure stories... It's <color=#dbc291ff>the Golden Land under the protection of Mnestia</color>!

t_ch: 那里是诗人、艺术家与追逐浪漫之人的天堂，一般主角都会在这里遇到自己的爱人，获得墨涅塔的赐福…此事在经典作品《金毯密传》中亦有记载。
t_en: It's a paradise for poets, artists, and those in search of romance. The main characters in these stories would meet their true love here and receive Mnestia's blessing... This is all recorded in the classic work Secret of the Golden Carpet.

t_ch: 说到《金毯密传》，那身为王子的男主与敌邦公主的私奔故事可谓精彩绝伦……
t_en: Speaking of the Secret of the Golden Carpet, that story of a prince eloping with the princess of an enemy state is truly a masterpiece...

t_ch: 克帖西芬开始滔滔不绝地介绍起这个故事，你如果再不问正事，估计她要跟你把全集都讲一遍。
t_en: Ctesiphona begins to endlessly talk about the story to you. If you don't get back to the matter at hand, she may end up recounting the entire epic.

t_ch: 该怎么去阿卡迪亚呢？
t_en: How do we get to Arcadia?

t_ch: 给个导航地址！
t_en: Drop the GPS location!

t_ch: 导航…哦，你是说「神启石镜」吧，这我可帮不了你。
t_en: GPS... Oh, you mean the "Stone Mirror of Revelation." I cannot help you there, I'm afraid.

t_ch: 奥赫玛有旅行社带团吗？
t_en: Does Okhema have travel tours?

t_ch: 旅游…我只在史书里听过这个词。这黑潮和疯狂眷属乱跑的年代没有旅游，只有以身探险的冒险者和商队。
t_en: Travel... I have only encountered that word in historical texts. There is no travel in this era, with the black tide and Titankin running rampant. We have only adventurers and merchants who roam the land at the risk of their own lives.

t_ch: 它的位置每个故事说的都不一样，有的说它在翁法罗斯的最中心，有的说它在法吉娜的海底…还有的说它在一群蝴蝶的背上，于高空中飘荡。
t_en: As for its location, every story says something different. Some say that it is at the center of Amphoreus. Some say it is in the ocean depths of Phagousa... Some even say that it is on the back of a flock of butterflies, floating high in the skies.

t_ch: 至于哪个是真的，我也不知道——毕竟都是故事嘛。
t_en: As for which is true, I have no idea... They're all stories, after all.

t_ch: 原来阿卡迪亚是个这么厉害的地方！但只有故事中的记述可能还不够，也没有问到关于帕里斯的事情…我们还是再找找其他人吧。
t_en: I didn't realize Arcadia was such an amazing place! Although, descriptions in stories probably still aren't enough, and we still know nothing about Paris... Let's go and find someone else to ask.

t_ch: ……敬仰墨涅塔的顾客确实喜欢所谓的「金丝礼服」，但用软金线来编织，不仅成本高昂，且穿者也会行动不便。不如用染色的丝线或布料来代替。
t_en: ...I know that those who revere Mnestia are fond of the so-called "golden thread dress," but weaving clothing out of malleable gold not only makes it very expensive, it also makes it very hard for the wearers to move in. Would it not be better to use dyed silk or cotton?

t_ch: 但人们都说墨涅塔的丝线就是黄金做的，如果用染色的丝线来替代，岂不是弄虚作假？
t_en: But everyone says that Mnestia's silk is made of gold. Using dyed silk would just be a forgery, wouldn't it?

t_ch: 与泰坦的造物相比，染色的线与金属的线都是仿神的赝品。执着于赝品的材质高低，而让衣物失去应有的美，对「浪漫」反倒更加不敬吧？
t_en: Compared to the creations of the Titans, dyed threads and metal threads are all merely works that imitate the work of the gods. Do you not think that obsessing over the quality of an imitation and ruining the clothes' intended beauty is a sign of disrespect to "romance" itself?

t_ch: 原来如此，感谢阿格莱雅老师的指点…啊，来客人了！
t_en: I see. Thank you for your guidance, knowledgeable Aglaea... Ah, a customer!

t_ch: 你好，贵客，想来找某件合身的行头，还是想来寻找某个问题的答案？
t_en: Hello, dear customer. Are you here for an item of clothing to suit your physique? Or could it be an answer to a question that you seek?

t_ch: 哇哦，一下就被看穿了。正好阿格莱雅见多识广，一定知道我们想要问的事情！
t_en: Woah. You saw right through me. I know you are a lady of great learning, Aglaea. I'm certain you know about what we seek!

t_ch: 你认识帕里斯吗？
t_en: Do you know Paris?

t_ch: 帕里斯？奥赫玛城中确实有过这样一位城民，应该是戍卫队的一员。
t_en: Paris? There is indeed no citizen of Okhema by this name. I imagine they are a member of the garrison.

t_ch: 那阿格莱雅小姐知道他现在身在何处吗？
t_en: Do you know where they are right now, Miss Aglaea?

t_ch: 这个…距离我上次听到这个名字已经有数十年了。之后我因事离开了圣城一段时间，当我再次回到奥赫玛，此人已不在城中了。
t_en: Well... It has been decades since I last heard that name. I was away from the holy city for a time and upon my return to Okhema, they were no longer here.

t_ch: 数十年？那他还活着吗？
t_en: Decades? Is he still alive?

t_ch: 也不能妄下定论，寿限在此世总是难以预测，毕竟或许存在逃避塞纳托斯抚摸的方法，也或许存在死亡无法触及的地方。
t_en: I cannot say for certain. The duration of a life is hard to predict in this world. Perhaps there exists some method to escape Thanatos' touch, or perhaps there is a land that death itself cannot reach.

t_ch: 你听说过阿卡迪亚吗？
t_en: Have you heard of Arcadia?

t_ch: 阿卡迪亚…这还真是个值得怀念的名字。
t_en: Arcadia... Now that is a name that stirs memories.

t_ch: 很久以前，这个名字曾流传于奥赫玛的街头巷尾，人们说那是一个被黄金之茧庇佑的理想之城，在纷争正盛的年代，人人都向往那个地方。
t_en: A long time ago, this name spread through the streets and alleys of Okhema. People said that it was an ideal city that was protected by the Chrysalis of Gold. In the era of strife, people flocked to it.

t_ch: 那…阿格莱雅知道该怎么找到这个地方吗？
t_en: Then... Do you know how to find this place, Aglaea?

t_ch: 抱歉，我并不知道。
t_en: My apologies, I do not.

t_ch: 和服装一样，传说往往也会应潮流来去。阿卡迪亚在如今已是「过时」的故事，许多墨涅塔的信徒都不知晓这个名字。
t_en: Much like clothing, legends are often things that come and go with the times. Arcadia is already a story of the "past." Many disciples of Mnestia don't even know this name.

t_ch: 或许应某个逸闻中所说，在这黑潮覆压的乱世，蝴蝶载着这座城飞到了谁也见不到的地方吧。
t_en: It could be the case, according to one story, that in this war-torn world savaged by the black tide, a flock of butterflies carried that city to a place that no one could see.

t_ch: 阿格莱雅女士——阿格莱雅女士！
t_en: Lady Aglaea! Lady Aglaea!

t_ch: 士兵，怎么了？
t_en: What's wrong, soldier?

t_ch: 伊阿宋带着物资回来了，但却不愿进城，说什么在等人——搞不好这小子把您的事情搞砸了，劳烦您亲自到场看看吧。
t_en: Iason has returned with supplies but refuses to enter the city. He says that he is waiting for someone... There is a chance that he failed in the task that you gave to him. Could I trouble you to come and see him yourself?

t_ch: …贵客，看来我们的话题需要暂时中断在这里了。愿你们能早些找到想要知晓的事情，我先告辞了。
t_en: ...Dear customer, it seems that we must leave our conversation here for now. I hope that you find the answers you seek soon. I bid you farewell.

t_ch: 这些口述和雕像小姐的记忆，应该足够让我们回到过去了，去找雕像小姐吧！
t_en: These descriptions, along with the statue lady's memories, should be enough for us to return to the past. Let's go and find the statue lady!

t_ch: 阿…卡…迪亚……
t_en: Ar... ca... dia...

t_ch: 一会儿未见，泰坦眷属似乎比之前更加颓靡了，能从声音中到感受到她的活力正在飘逝……
t_en: In the time that you were gone, it seems that the Titankin has become even more desperate than before. You can sense from her voice that her vitality is slowly fading...

t_ch: 她看起来不太好…
t_en: She doesn't look too good...

t_ch: 能救一下吗？
t_en: Can we save her?

t_ch: 人家不知道怎么修理眷属，她这样子也去不了其他地方……
t_en: I don't know how to repair Titankin. She can't go anywhere else in this state, either...

t_ch: 她能吃消耗品吗？
t_en: Can she take consumables?

t_ch: 这…人家没看到她的嘴长在哪里呀。
t_en: Uhh... I don't even know where her mouth would be.

t_ch: 我们现在能做的，也只有快点了解她的内心，帮她找到想要见到的人了。
t_en: All we can do for now is try to understand what she wants and help her meet the person she longs for.

t_ch: 走吧，伙伴，让我们看看阿卡迪亚和那位帕里斯到底在哪——
t_en: Let's go, partner. Let's find out just where this Paris guy and this Arcadia place are.

t_ch: 迷迷挥手打开了通向过往的门扉……
t_en: Mem waves their hand and opens a door to the past...

t_ch: 这里是纷争浇灌出的河口，这河用了敌人流淌的鲜血，而不是天上来的雨点，去湿润无休止的战场。
t_en: This is the river mouth irrigated by strife. It is not raindrops that flow from this river, but the blood of enemies that dampens the field of unceasing wars.

t_ch: 我诞生的地方是尼卡多利的城邦，漂泊于永夜天帷之下的堡垒。这里的人不知何为山海。他们的山是战场的余烬堆积的灰山，他们的海是战败敌人流淌的血海。
t_en: I was born in the city of Nikador, a castle that floats below the Evernight Veil. The people here know nothing of mountains or oceans. Their mountains are piles of burning ash that litter the battlefield, and their oceans are a sea of blood that flows from their enemies.

t_ch: 是的，悬锋城便是这样的地方，而我的父亲——如果造主能称之为父亲的话——乃是带来纷争的尼卡多利。
t_en: That's right. That is exactly the kind of place that Castrum Kremnos is. My father — if you can call a creator father — was Nikador themself, bringer of strife.

t_ch: 我的兄弟姐妹们说，它曾告诉他们的先祖：宁战死，毋荣归。唯有死于战场之上，成为纷争的祭品，才是值得骄傲的命运。
t_en: My siblings used to tell me of a prophecy that Nikador had told their ancestors: "Valorous death before glorious return." Only offering your life to the battlefield and becoming a sacrifice to strife is a fate worthy of pride.

t_ch: 凯旋尚且不能为傲，败退更是玷污悬锋的恶行。它曾告诉它们的先祖：悬锋上的泥垢，必须用血洗去。
t_en: There is no pride in victory, and defeat is an even more disgraceful act that tarnishes Kremnos. The god told their ancestors: "The filth of Kremnos must be washed clean in blood."

t_ch: 悬锋城督政官上。
t_en: The Ephor of Castrum Kremnos appears.

t_ch: 谁是战场上蒙羞而归的败者？谁是误入鲜血蜜酿的飞蝇？谁是悬锋上应被洗涤入血河的污垢？谁辜负了纷争的尼卡多利之授命？
t_en: Who is the loser who returned from the battlefield in shame? Who is the fly that accidentally flew into the mead of fresh blood? Who is the dirt on Kremnos that must be washed in the river of blood? Who has failed the duty of Nikador of strife?

t_ch: 是你——它亲自铸造的眷属。你的血统尊贵如角斗浴血的胜者，你的使命高尚如破腹献肠的祭司。但你却辜负了你的命运，在对奥赫玛军的突袭中失利，败退而归。
t_en: It is you... The Titankin that they created themselves. Your bloodlines are as noble as a gladiator's, raising their weapon high in victory. Your mission is as noble as the priest that pierces the belly and viscera of offerings. Yet you have failed your destiny, as you were defeated in your ambush of the Okhema army and returned vanquished.

t_ch: 失败的纷争利器，负罪的悬锋子民。残躯已无法继续征战，荣耀的悬锋会将你背负耻辱的躯壳粉碎，让死亡的手来抹除悬锋的污垢。
t_en: You have failed as an instrument of strife, and you bring guilt on the citizens of Kremnos. Your broken body is capable of battle no more. The glory of Kremnos will smash your body that bears such shame and allow the hands of death to purify this stain on Kremnos.

t_ch: 尼卡多利的孩子们，将你们受辱的姊妹带往监牢，让她与另一个懦夫一同等待命运的裁决吧。
t_en: Children of Nikador! Take your humiliated sister to the prison and have her await her fated punishment alongside her fellow coward.

t_ch: 她没有发出声音，她不觉得苦痛，反倒认为理所应当。
t_en: She makes no sound, and she feels no pain. She knows she is the one to blame.

t_ch: 开裂的箭镞重回炉床，死去的战士埋入坟场，这名为命运的规则理应是这样。
t_en: As broken arrows are cast in rebirth, and fallen soldiers return to the earth. These are but the rules of fate.

t_ch: 兄弟，你说我们为啥要把同类和敌军的俘虏关在一起啊。
t_en: Brother, do you know why we must imprison our own kind alongside enemy soldiers?

t_ch: 真不想回答你这种没脑子的种类，那当然是因为她打了败仗，比那打了胜仗的俘虏还可悲。
t_en: I don't want to sit and answer your brainless questions. Obviously it's because she was defeated in battle. That is more shameful than an enemy soldier that battled victoriously.

t_ch: 那兄弟，要是咱们以后打了败仗，是不是也要挨悬锋城的铁矛啊？
t_en: Does that mean, brother... That if we are defeated in battle, we will suffer the spear of Castrum Kremnos, too?

t_ch: 怕啥？咱们是后方，背锅也是前线背…等会儿，这是谁在偷听呢。
t_en: What are you so worried about? We're backline forces, and the frontline is always going to be the scapegoat... Wait, someone is listening.

t_ch: 我是来找人的。
t_en: I'm here to see someone.

t_ch: 我是来劫狱的。
t_en: I came to break someone out.

t_ch: 我是来打架的。
t_en: I'm here for a fight.

t_ch: 是入侵者！兄弟，咱们快阻止他！
t_en: Intruder! Brother, we have to stop them!

t_ch: 等等，我怎么觉得这家伙不太对劲……
t_en: Wait. There's something not quite right about them...

t_ch: 或许是因为有着尼卡多利的血统，天谴哲人敏锐地察觉到你身上散发出的强者气息——他们并不知道，他的神在未来都被你轰杀了呀！
t_en: Perhaps they carry the blood of Nikador? The Furiae Philosopher keenly senses the aura of strength emanating from your body... They have no idea that their god will be slain by you in the future!

t_ch: 你可想清楚了？
t_en: Have you seen sense?

t_ch: 我两个都同样的揍啊。
t_en: I will destroy you both.

t_ch: 战。
t_en: Fight.

t_ch: 等会儿！我觉得咱俩拦不住这家伙，要不就当没看见……
t_en: Wait! I don't think just us two can stop them. Why don't we just pretend we never saw them...

t_ch: 啊？但我们这岂不是当了逃兵？
t_en: Huh? Would that not make us deserters?

t_ch: 傻帽，要是打输了，咱俩也得被关在下面！
t_en: You fool. If we're defeated, we'll end up locked up anyway!

t_ch: 再说了，除非通过「神性」的力量，否则没人能打开牢房的门…快走吧！
t_en: What's more, no one can unlock the prison gates without some form of "divine" power anyway... Let's get out of here!

t_ch: （雕像中的神性已被提取，用它去开启牢房的机关吧……）
t_en: (The statue's divinity has already been extracted. Use it to open the prison gate mechanism...)

t_ch: 呵呵，人们常说悬锋城是个粗野的地方，但看来并非如此——他们不仅为战俘带来了一位朋友，这位朋友还是个美丽的姑娘！
t_en: Hehe. They all say that Castrum Kremnos is a rough and boorish land, but I see that is not the case... Not only did they bring a friend for us prisoners of war, this friend happens to be a beautiful young girl!

t_ch: …我不是朋友，只是因为没能送你们全军踏入冥河，而不得不与你共赴冥河的败者。而我也不是姑娘，只是铠甲用了女子的模样。
t_en: ...I am not a friend. I am merely a failure that must swim the River of Souls alongside you as I was unable to send your entire army to its depths. Nor am I a young girl. My armor was simply cast in the shape of a girl.

t_ch: 墨涅塔在上，这石铸的身躯兼具妇人的健美与雕塑的精妙，那不屈与坚韧的美映照在你的脸庞。要我说，你便是这城邦中最美的姑娘。
t_en: Great Mnestia, this statue combines the fine physique of a woman with the finesse of sculpture. Your unyielding and tenacious beauty is reflected in your face. If you ask me, you are the most beautiful girl in this city.

t_ch: …轻口薄舌的流贼、恶痞、败寇，莫要用谵语侮辱悬锋城的战士。
t_en: ...Sharp-tongued thieves, scoundrels, and failed bandits should not seek to insult a warrior of Castrum Kremnos with their wild ravings.

t_ch: 姑娘为我想的称号还真是丰富，但我还是更希望你叫我的名字——<color=#dbc291ff>帕里斯</color>。
t_en: You have so many nicknames for me, miss. But I would prefer you to call me by my name: <color=#dbc291ff>Paris</color>.

t_ch: 帕里斯…
t_en: Paris...

t_ch: 原来是这小子…
t_en: So this is the guy...

t_ch: 哥们说话有股味儿…
t_en: His lines are pretty cringy...

t_ch: 等等，是谁的声音？
t_en: Wait, who said that?

t_ch: 有人闯进来了——穿着不是悬锋城的士兵，你是何人？
t_en: Someone has broken in... You do not wear the armor of a Castrum Kremnos soldier. Who are you?

t_ch: 我是来救你们的。
t_en: I came to rescue you two.

t_ch: 太好了！雅努斯都不给开的门你给开了，你比雅努斯还牛！
t_en: Oh felicitations! You opened the door that not even Janus would! You are greater than Janus themselves!

t_ch: 我是来看戏的。
t_en: I'm here to watch the show.

t_ch: 伙计，你说的戏总不能是我们被处刑的表演吧？这可不好看，放了我，带你去圣城看好的！
t_en: My friend, by show... you do not mean the performance of our execution, do you? That would be a terrible display. Release me and I will take you to a real show in the holy city!

t_ch: 我是银河球棒侠！
t_en: I am the Galactic Baseballer!

t_ch: …什么玩意儿？
t_en: ...What the heck?

t_ch: 哦！我知道，那些大英雄们都喜欢给自己起各种各样的称号——你一定也是外出冒险的英雄吧，快救救我们！
t_en: Oh! I know! Great heroes love to give themselves all sorts of titles... You must be a hero out on a great adventure, too? Quick, you must save us!

t_ch: 毫无尊严的懦夫，要逃走你就自己逃吧。
t_en: You dishonorable coward. If you wish to escape, do it alone.

t_ch: 什么？你是不是打仗把脑袋打坏了——如果你的构造有脑袋的话。
t_en: What? Have you battled so much that your brain got knocked silly? ...If you have a brain in there, that is.

t_ch: 你没听他们说要把我们变成祭品，切成丁抛到铸魂的金血里，平息尼卡多利的愤怒吗？
t_en: Have you not heard that they plan to make us sacrifices? To dice our bodies and cast them into the soul-melting golden blood to quell the rage of Nikador?

t_ch: 那又如何。身为尼卡多利的造物，战败便是对吾父最大的亵渎，理应投身死潮向其谢罪。
t_en: So what? As a creation of Nikador, defeat is the greatest blasphemy against my father. It is only natural that I should cast my body into the waves of death to apologize for my failure.

t_ch: 得了吧，它要是看到别人变成碎块下锅就能消气，就应该是掌管厨艺的泰坦！
t_en: Are you serious? If they are the type to be appeased by seeing others diced and cast into a pot, would that not make them the Titan of cooking!?

t_ch: 尼卡多利生腌！
t_en: Nikador raw pickles!

t_ch: 悬锋特色火锅！
t_en: Kremnos' famous hotpot!

t_ch: 天谴之矛串串香！
t_en: Lance of Fury skewers!

t_ch: 唔，听起来挺好吃的？
t_en: Hm, that actually sounds good?

t_ch: 你们…竟然敢在悬锋城中侮辱尼卡多利——守卫！有闯入者……
t_en: You... You dare insult Nikador within the walls of Castrum Kremnos... Guards! There is an intruder...

t_ch: 砰！
t_en: Crash!

t_ch: 帕里斯用臂铠砸在天谴猎手的头盔上，顿时让本就负创的她昏死了过去。
t_en: Paris smashes his gauntlet against the Furiae Archer's helmet, causing the already injured Titankin to pass out immediately.

t_ch: 我们可不能拒绝命运赐予生机，就像我们也不能拒绝命运赐予的失败一样——伙计，她这副石铸的身子可不轻，待会就麻烦你搭把手了。
t_en: We cannot decline an opportunity to live granted to us by fate! Just as we cannot escape a failure fated by destiny... My friend, this stone statue is by no means light. I will need you to help me lift her.

t_ch: 男人托起天谴猎手的身体，吃力地往出口走去……
t_en: The man lifts the body of the Furiae Archer and struggles towards the exit...

t_ch: 无耻的懦夫，蒙羞的败者，以尼卡多利之名——绝不允许你们踏出悬锋城半步。
t_en: Shameless cowards, useless failures. In the name of Nikador, I will not allow you to step one foot outside Castrum Kremnos!

t_ch: 坏了坏了，伙计，这可怎么办呢？
t_en: Oh crap, oh crap. My friend, what do we do?

t_ch: 居然还想逃跑…看来只有在献祭前切断祭品的四肢，才能让胆怯者安然接受命运。
t_en: You're still trying to escape...? I see. It seems that only when a sacrifice's limbs have all been sliced off clean that these cowards will finally accept their fate.

t_ch: 接受不了一点。
t_en: I cannot accept any of that.

t_ch: 我先切切你的。
t_en: Not if I slice yours off first.

t_ch: 这尺度有点不合规了。
t_en: That's pushing the family-friendly rating a bit too far.

t_ch: 说得好！看你的语气肯定不是泛泛之辈！就靠你对付他了，英雄！
t_en: That's the spirit! That is not the tongue of a mere citizen! Take them out for us, hero!

t_ch: 别跑——掠夺祭品的异乡人，一起成为吾父蜜酿的配料吧！
t_en: Stop right there! Outlanders that plundered the sacrifices to our lord! You will become seasonings in my father's honey brew!

t_ch: 哼哼，我的伙伴厉害吧，快来夸…等等，他们人呢？
t_en: Hmph. Isn't my partner amazing? You can say it... Wait, where did they go?

t_ch: 好像已经跑了。
t_en: Looks like they already ran off.

t_ch: 我嘞个逃兵。
t_en: They really are deserters.

t_ch: 好一个卖撤溜。
t_en: Talk about bailing.

t_ch: 这个帕里斯也太不讲义气了！我们还没问关于阿卡迪亚的事情呢！
t_en: This Paris guy knows nothing about loyalty! We still didn't get to ask him anything about Arcadia!

t_ch: 算了，记忆的气味飘向了奥赫玛的方向，我们继续让记忆向前吧——
t_en: Forget it. The memory fragrance is floating towards Okhema. Let's continue following the memory...

t_ch: 伙伴，一起继续探索往世吧，雕像小姐和伊阿宋还等着我们呢。
t_en: Partner, let's keep exploring the past! The statue lady and Iason are still waiting for us!

t_ch: 好，出发吧。
t_en: Alright, let's go.

t_ch: 再等等。
t_en: One sec.

t_ch: 好吧，快点哦！
t_en: Okay. Be quick!

t_ch: 将我的性命从灰黯之手夺回的勇士，你何时才能从纷争掀起的雾潮中归还呢？
t_en: Oh warrior who stole my life back from the Hand of Shadow. When will you return from the tides wrought by strife?

t_ch: 俄诺涅元老，您的明智是洗涤云崖的溪流，不应让这城外的尘埃染了这水池的清澈。
t_en: Elder Oenone, your wisdom is the stream that cleanses Dawncloud. You mustn't allow a speck of dust from outside the great city to infect the clarity of this pool.

t_ch: 戍卫长，莫要调笑我。若无未归的英雄相救，我这摊池水早已沦入黑潮了。
t_en: Captain, do not mock me. If it were not for the soldier who is yet to return, this pool would have long since been engulfed by the black tide.

t_ch: 但先前抵御敌军的将士已然凯旋，您所等待的那位帕里斯不在阵中，已是凶多吉少。
t_en: But the soldiers who fought off the enemy have already returned triumphant. That Paris that you wait for has not yet returned, which means his fate is likely one of misfortune.

t_ch: 唉！若是如此，我又有何脸面于云崖之上安然度日？如果他战死沙场，我便辞去元老职务，久居于城墙之前为他悼念。
t_en: Agh! If that is so, how can I bring myself to return to Dawncloud and live my days in peace? If he fell on the sands of the battlefield, I shall resign my post as the elder and spend the rest of my days before the city walls in remembrance of him.

t_ch: 呵，您如此执迷，不知是塔兰顿的砝码压住了您，还是墨涅塔的金丝缠住了您？
t_en: Hah. This obsession of yours... I cannot tell if you are crushed under the weights of Talanton or bound by the golden thread of Mnestia?

t_ch: 你…戍卫长，先去看看远处靠近城门的是何人吧。
t_en: You... Captain, go and see who that is that approaches the city gate.

t_ch: 帕里斯上。天谴猎手上。
t_en: Paris appears, as does the Furiae Archer.

t_ch: 是帕里斯！我的恩人！赞美命运三泰坦！
t_en: It's Paris! My savior! Praised be the Three Titans of Fate!

t_ch: 俄诺涅女士，您还是如初见时那样美丽！菲罗克长官也还是那么阴郁，看来胜利也扫不去您心中的愁绪。
t_en: Lady Oenone, you are as ravishing as the day I met you! Captain Philoc, your face is as overcast as ever. I see even victory cannot rid you of the melancholy in your heart.

t_ch: 帕里斯…看来是你的言谈令塞纳托斯都感到厌烦，让它把你从冥河里丢了出来。
t_en: Paris... I see even Thanatos grew weary of your incessant ramblings and cast you out from the River of Souls themselves.

t_ch: 等等，你身旁的是献给奥赫玛的战利品吗？
t_en: Wait, do you bring with you a spoil of war as an offering to Okhema?

t_ch: 不，她也是从天谴之矛下逃离的可怜人，请收留她吧。
t_en: No, she is an escapee of the Lance of Fury that deserves our mercy. Please, you must offer her shelter.

t_ch: 收留？荒谬！你知道有多少同胞曾死于这些眷属的手下吗？
t_en: Shelter? Preposterous! Do you know how many of our kind have died at the hands of these Titankin?

t_ch: 等等，戍卫长，全世之座下可容天地，莫要让你的仇恨遮蔽了它头顶的黎明——我以元老的名义为她担保，带她进城吧。
t_en: Wait, Captain. The Throne of Worlds accepts all. Do not allow your hatred to blind you to the dawn above... By my name as the elder, I will vouch for her. Bring her into the city.

t_ch: 元老，请您三思……
t_en: Elder, you must reconsider...

t_ch: 感谢俄诺涅元老！您的善良令刻法勒的胸怀显得更加宽广，令这圣城更加高贵！
t_en: Thank you, Elder Oenone! Your kindness broadens even the heart of Kephale and crowns this holy city with even greater nobility!

t_ch: 不用谢我，帕里斯。若不是你挽回了我险些断于黑潮的命运之丝，我的这份善良也无处可施……
t_en: No need to thank me, Paris. If you had not saved my thread of fate from being cut off by the black tide, this kindness would have already been lost...

t_ch: 看啊，你所守护的人和部下挽着敌人的手步入城中，而那被挽起的手可能在前日刚刚射杀了你的父兄。
t_en: Do you see? The person and subordinate whom you protect has marched into the city hand-in-hand with the enemy. Your fathers and brothers could have just lost their lives to those very hands a few days ago.

t_ch: 我们将士为元老们舍命拼杀，而元老却因情欲所惑，将我们的仇敌视作宾客……
t_en: We soldiers risk our lives fighting to protect the elders, yet they are tempted by lust and throw open the doors to our enemies...

t_ch: 是啊，在金丝的牵掣下，塔兰顿的天秤也难免倾斜，雅努斯的指引也失了方向……
t_en: That's right! With the interference of the golden thread, even Talanton's scale become tilted, and direction from Janus loses its way...

t_ch: 不会吧，伟大的大工匠，难道您修不好吗？
t_en: Surely not. Grand Craftsman, are you saying that you cannot repair her?

t_ch: 能。材料，没有。大地的石料，纷争的金血，理性的种子——一无所有。
t_en: Can. Materials, have not. Earth's stones, golden blood of strife, seeds of reason — nothing there is.

t_ch: 唉，怎么又都是泰坦们的东西，他们这些家伙还真是爱难为凡人。
t_en: Ah! Why are all those things derived from the Titans. Oh, how they rejoice at the suffering of mortals.

t_ch: 放弃吧…不要再玷污我残存的荣耀了…让我投身命定的冥流……
t_en: Just give up... Do not further sully what little honor I have remaining... Let me give myself to my destiny in the underworld...

t_ch: 姑娘，你都快没气了，就少说点丧气话吧。
t_en: Miss, your strength is fading. Do not waste it on pessimism.

t_ch: 让我想想办法…大工匠，这东西是？
t_en: Let me think of a way... Grand Craftsman, what is this?

t_ch: 算碑，圣物，欧洛尼斯的。解开预言，改变时空，很难。
t_en: Calculation tablet, divine object, Oronyx's it is. Unravel prophecy, change time and space, hard it is.

t_ch: 哦！我知道这东西，雅努萨波利斯的神棍最喜欢玩的玩具，应该也能修好她身上的窟窿！
t_en: Oh! I know this! This is Janusopolis' astrologers' favorite toy! This should be able to repair the holes in her body!

t_ch: 你不懂，无法使用。
t_en: Understand, you do not. Use it, you can not.

t_ch: 嗐，奥赫玛这么大，就找不到一个玩得转这玩意儿的人吗？
t_en: Hey, with a city as vast as Okhema, are you sure we can find no one that can make this thing spin?

t_ch: 我玩得转。
t_en: I can make it spin.

t_ch: 欧洛尼斯亲传弟子来了。
t_en: Disciple of Oronyx, at your service.

t_ch: 国服第一算碑玩家申请出战！
t_en: Calculation tablet national champion, ready for battle!

t_ch: 啊！是之前的恩人，没想到你真的能打过泰坦眷属…不对，我就知道你一定能脱身！
t_en: Ah! My savior from before! I never expected you would defeat the Titankin... I mean, I just knew you'd make it out alive!

t_ch: 既然你懂算碑，那这次也要拜托你帮忙了——别担心，我帕里斯绝不会让你白忙这么多次，事后定有重谢！
t_en: As you understand this thing, I must ask you for help once more... Fear not, Paris will not let your efforts be in vain again, and they will be greatly appreciated!

t_ch: 总感觉这家伙说话不怎么靠谱…算了，为了雕像小姐，就让我们帮帮他吧。
t_en: I can't help but feel like this guy is shady... Whatever, let's help him for the statue lady's sake.

t_ch: 太厉害了，恩人！我在奥赫玛这么久居然不知道有你这号文武双全的英雄！
t_en: My savior, you are truly amazing! After all my years in Okhema, I cannot fathom that I knew not of a hero as both wise and strong as you!

t_ch: 小嘴儿还挺甜。
t_en: You're a real charmer.

t_ch: 待会报酬多给点就行。
t_en: Just pay me extra.

t_ch: 你多活几十年就知道了。
t_en: Live a few more centuries and you'll see.

t_ch: 你们…为什么要救我？
t_en: Why... Why are you trying to save me?

t_ch: 我想从你身上知道一些事情。
t_en: I need some information from you.

t_ch: 你未来会有我需要的东西。
t_en: You will have something I need one day.

t_ch: 为了得到崇高道德的赞许。
t_en: To receive the Praise of High Morals.

t_ch: 但你先扪心自问一下，为了得到赞许而行使的善行，值得崇高道德的瞥视吗？
t_en: You can't help but stop to ask yourself... If something is done in order to receive praise, is it worthy of being looked at as highly moral?

t_ch: 确实不太好…
t_en: Now that you said it...

t_ch: 是吧，既然你自己也没想清楚自己的动机，崇高道德也很难对你加以信赖。
t_en: Right? As you were not even clear of your motivation, the high morality finds it hard to place their faith in you.

t_ch: 当然值得！
t_en: Of course it is!

t_ch: 是吧，君子论迹不论心，你的果断与自信值得崇高道德…在这里或许是塔兰顿的奖赏！
t_en: Right? It's about the means above the ends. Your decisiveness and confidence are worthy of high moral praise... Perhaps this is a reward from Talanton themself!

t_ch: 呵，果然另有所图。那你呢，叫做帕里斯的士兵。
t_en: Hmph, I knew you had ulterior motives. Then what of you, soldier by the name of Paris?

t_ch: 墨涅塔的神言说过：不能对美丽的事物的逝去熟视无睹。懂我意思吧，美丽的姑娘。
t_en: The divine declaration of Mnestia states: One must not turn a blind eye as beauty dies. You understand what I mean, don't you, beautiful girl?

t_ch: 你这人真是……
t_en: You are exhausting...

t_ch: 不过还有另一个原因，那便是我对你的言行感到不快！
t_en: However, I have another reason. Your words and actions displease me!

t_ch: 面对不公的死亡，你不选择抗争，甚至不愿选择挣扎。我要救下你的命，就是要向你证明：命运可不是不能战胜的东西！
t_en: You chose not to fight back even in the face of an unfair execution — you would not even put up a struggle. I saved your life to prove to you: Destiny is something that can be defeated!

t_ch: …愚蠢。
t_en: ...Foolishness.

t_ch: 行啦，既然来了奥赫玛，逛云石市集可比骂我蠢有意思多了，我带你看看吧！
t_en: Okay, okay. Well, as we are in Okhema, a browse of Marmoreal Market would be far more entertaining than sitting around calling me foolish all day. I'll take you!

t_ch: 这还有个人呢！
t_en: I'm here too, you know!

t_ch: 这就进约会剧情了？
t_en: Are we going into a romance plot now?

t_ch: 能先把我奖励结了吗？
t_en: Can I just take my rewards and go?

t_ch: 哎呀，不好意思，忘了我们的恩人——一起来吧，你的报酬也在这边。
t_en: Oh, sorry. I forgot about you, our savior. Come with us, I shall take care of your payment there, too.

t_ch: 嗨，梅塔涅拉婆婆，最近生意还好吗？
t_en: Hey, Granny Metanella. How's business?

t_ch: 天呐，是小帕里斯！你还活着！婆婆都准备好给塞纳托斯为你摆渡的祭品了！
t_en: Heavens! Young Paris! You're alive! I was all prepared to give offerings to Thanatos for your safe passage!

t_ch: 哎哟，那我可真是谢谢您嘞，现在我活着回来了，不如就把这祭品送我吧。
t_en: Oh, then I thank you for your kindness. Seeing as I have returned alive, how about giving these offerings to me instead?

t_ch: 呵呵，行啊，拿着吧——等等，婆婆是花了眼吗，你身后怎么站着一个尼卡多利的眷属！？
t_en: Hah, fine, take them. Hold on, am I seeing things? Is that a Nikador Titankin behind you!?

t_ch: 放心吧，婆婆，她是咱们奥赫玛的客人。
t_en: Fear not, granny. She is a guest of Okhema.

t_ch: 这样呀…不好意思，欢迎你来到奥赫玛，客人。
t_en: I see... My apologies, welcome to Okhema, dear guest.

t_ch: 你不害怕我吗？
t_en: Do you not fear me?

t_ch: 呵呵，这里是奥赫玛，在刻法勒的庇护下，没什么好怕的。
t_en: Hah, here in the land of Okhema, we are under the protection of Kephale. There is nothing to fear here.

t_ch: 婆婆我这辈子没出过城，所以悬锋城的事情都是听来的——你们不种地只打仗，估计连水果都只能吃抢来的吧？
t_en: I have never left this city in my life, so all I know of Castrum Kremnos is what I've heard... like how you live only to fight and do not farm the land. I imagine the only fruit you've ever tasted is what you have pillaged?

t_ch: 来了奥赫玛，就让婆婆请你吃个够！来点苹果？还是麦迪特瑞恩蜜果？
t_en: You are in Okhema now, let me treat you to a feast! Would you care for an apple? Maybe a Mediterrana fig?

t_ch: 我不需要。
t_en: I don't need it.

t_ch: 别推辞了，梅塔涅拉婆婆平时可抠门得很。
t_en: You must not refuse her generosity. Granny Metanella is normally extremely stingy.
